steps.title: Support shared microhabitats
steps.content: The underlined claim says male and female A. fasciatus share the same sponge. The graph must therefore show both sexes present in the same sponge microhabitat, not just one sex or sharks whose sex is unknown.

steps.title: Reject one-sex data
steps.content: Choice A includes males and undetermined sharks, not females. Choice B gives only male sharks in Sponge 1. Choice D gives only female sharks in Sponge 1. The remaining choice shows that each of the three sponges contained at least one male and at least one female, which directly supports the claim that males and females share sponge microhabitats. The answer is C.
